level_signals: Owns fraud product strategy across all money movement rails
Direct ownership of real-time systems and detection logic

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or higher, ideally in a technical, finance, or quantitative field.
4+ years of Product Management experience: With a focus on fraud, risk, payments, or FinTech.
must_haves.qualifier: focus on fraud, risk, payments, or FinTech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You possess a strong understanding of common fraud attack vectors and the strategic product solutions required to mitigate them.
You understand the nuances of different payment rails (ACH, Wires, Card) and how fraudsters exploit the "settlement gap" in each.

You are comfortable diving into SQL or data visualization tools to understand why a specific rule fired and how to tighten the net without catching good users. You can synthesize inputs and data from multiple sources to build a strategic rationale that advocates for long-term customer value.
You don't just want to write a rule; you want to build a repeatable framework for how we authorize money movement across the entire enterprise.
You can distill complex concepts into easily understood product strategy and requirements, tailoring your message to engineering, data science, and business stakeholders.
A proactive bias for ownership, capable of taking initiative on product outcomes, finding scrappy ways around roadblocks, and driving multiple key results.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience tuning specific detection systems (like Oscilar, Datavisor, Flowable, or internal decision engines).
Direct experience with the unique risk challenges of physical Check fraud and irreversible real-time payments.
Experience working closely with Data Science and Machine Learning Engineers to operationalize real-time scoring and transaction monitoring models.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska

scope.surface: Fraud product strategy for all money movement rails at SoFi — ACH, Wires, Debit Card, Credit Card, Checks, P2P, International Remittances, Crypto
primary_metrics_inferred: Sub-second detection latency for real-time rails
core_responsibilities: Define and execute the product strategy for transaction authorization and monitoring, both in-app and out-of-app
Enable the rules and ML models that decide in milliseconds whether a transaction is safe, suspicious, or fraudulent
Build and iterate on velocity policies and transaction-time policy checks that adapt based on member risk and behavior
Own the logic for assessing member eligibility to attempt a transaction and strategically issuing step-up challenges
Tune detection systems to maximize loss prevention while minimizing False Positives
Own fraud product strategy for all transactional money movement rails including ACH, Wires, Debit Card, Credit Card, Checks, P2P, International Remittances and Crypto
Partner with Fraud Products Lead, Account Security team, and Fraud Data team
Manage and optimize key success metrics including Gross/Net Fraud Loss, detection latency, and False Positive Rate

green_flags: Clear and specific ownership area — all transactional money movement rails
Explicit success metrics defined in the JD (BPS, latency, FPR)
Role sits at intersection of Data Science, Engineering, and Payments — high-impact cross-functional mandate
Specificity of fraud domains (ACH, Wires, Check, RTP, Crypto) signals mature fraud org
Nice-to-haves are genuinely additive, not disguised must-haves
red_flags: Compensation range referenced but not disclosed — 'base pay range listed below' appears to be a template error with no actual range in the JD
Location type is entirely unclear — no city, remote policy, or hybrid guidance stated in the JD body
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Hawaii and Alaska explicitly excluded from remote work, implying some remote eligibility exists, but no further remote policy is stated — ambiguous
